title: How does Anno feel about Evas success
display_title: How does Anno feel about Evas success
wikitext:
[]: How does Anno feel about Eva's success?             Quote Post by Reiggae » Tue Jun 24, 2014 7:06 pm Anno said a while ago that he didn't understand Evangelion's popularity because "all the characters are sick". Eva has gotten way more popular since he said that and has become more of a brand nowadays. How do you think Anno feels about all the merchandise? Eva was a very personal project for him. Is he okay with it being (for lack of a better word) milked like that?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Jun 24, 2014 9:03 pm I Don't think it is being milked, trust me there are alotta milked things out there and Eva is not one of them. Anyway i think he welcomes it. There are videos all around internet showing him sharing his artistic ideas and how he approaches things. He gets a lot of recognition for being deep and thoughtful. Im pretty sure most people based there opinion on him from what they see in the Evangelion franchise, bc of the franchises popularity around the world now.

[]:             Quote Post by Reiggae » Tue Jun 24, 2014 9:11 pm Milked wasn't the right term. I'm talking more about how there are a lot of merchandise and product placements that seem unfitting to a show like Eva. (not that it's bad, just that it could be seen as insulting to Anno) Example of what I'm talking about: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Jun 24, 2014 9:23 pm O well advertising is everywhere. You know that movie the avengers, yea the one with the superheros that made like a billion $ at the box office. Well i rewatched it yesterday for like the 4th time and half way through the movie i started noticing a shit-load of advertising that i didn't notice before. Eva in Japan is what the avengers and batman are in America. So yea im not too suprised to see all these labels and adverting slapped on there. Anno , i don't he minds all the endorsements he gets, bc he doing what he loves and making money doing it. "Money can't buy happiness, but it sure as hell doesn't hurt"

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Jun 24, 2014 9:55 pm It's a bit ironic considering Evangelion was trying to deconstruct the 'giant robot' cashcow and flip it on it's head. In turn the franchise has now become one of the most heavily marketed & merchandised show around. One of the things I don't think Anno ever wanted it to become. It does at times seem that the story of Shinji's personal journey of finding happiness in life (and learning to love and accept yourself) gets lost within all the Rei/Asuka figures in bikinis and love pillows.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:03 pm I would go as far to say that Anno has done some of the milking himself (Rebuild)

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:08 pm ^ End of Evangelion came out in 1997 while 1.0 came out in '07 How is a 10 year gap without any new content (not including the sperratic release of the manga) milking?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:09 pm ^ I don't agree with that being milking bc it took him 10 years to come back and do rebuild after EoE. If he wanted to milk it, he would have already announced a season 2 after he was with rebuild. The bikinis and Asuka/Rei pillows, well thats jus Japan and marketing for you.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:19 pm I remember Anno once calling a kid an idiot because he spend all of his college money on Eva stuff. Can't seem find the source..

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:20 pm ^Lol. I need to meet this college kid, he sounds a lot like me.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:21 pm It sounds like we need to get Anno and George Lucas into a cage match.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:24 pm Ha. The conversation would probably be like "My incest ship is better than your incest ship" Let me know if you catch my drift.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 24, 2014 10:37 pm Well, narratively and thematically speaking, Anno still tipped the "giant robot” aesthetic on its head. I don’t think he was intentionally trying to make Eva in such a way that was financially irresponsible or in a way that wouldn’t make money. I’m sure both he and the studio wanted to see some profit from this endeavor. No one at GAINAX wanted to relive the days of producing Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water , full of 18-hour work days, minimal staff, and tight budgets, so they tried their best not to. What was really happening was that excessive marketing simply wasn’t on the forefront of the original Eva Project. (Arguably still sin’t, seeing as how it took about a year after 3.0’s theatrical release before we finally got a model of Asuka’s and Mari's second plug suits in the film.) As far as I can tell, Anno simply didn’t want the toy manufacturers to get in the way of Eva designs. Obviously toys would be made. It was never reported that GAINAX withheld merchandising rights from toy manufactures. They only avoided the creative input from the toy manufacturers. Yes, this could have resulted in toy companies to dismiss the idea of producing more complex models, but they were in no way forbidden to do so. This wasn’t like Bill Waterson keeping companies from the merchandising rights to "Calvin and Hobbes.” It was more like Charles Shultz not wanting to be told how Snoopy was going to look. Anno simply made sure that he and his creative team could have more freedom in the design process of both the Eva Units and the character designs, even if it meant a backlash from toy companies. (A girl in bandages wasn’t thought of as extremely marketable in the early to mid 90’s. Not until Anno accidentally proved that notion wrong, anyway.)

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jun 25, 2014 1:24 am Well he doesn't complain about the millions of dollars in royalties ...we can guarantee that.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 25, 2014 3:19 am Dataprime wrote: I remember Anno once calling a kid an idiot because he spend all of his college money on Eva stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Wed Jun 25, 2014 4:27 am Well im glad i only have the Plat. DVD's. No figures, no other merchandise ... only the movies and Blueray's. Okay i have to admit i could spend the money for some merchandise but my gf wouldn't allow this kind of kink. I think im not even into this ... its like with One Piece ... some figures are awesome ... but what is the point of buying them and put it around your room? I admire Eva (esp. NGE+EoE) for what it is: super-selfcoaching entertaiment - with one of the best fandom's in IMHO and a really construable story for lots of Fanfictions. I would say this is the most plain way to admire/worship the Evangelion-franchise. It is like a good book you can't stop talking about if you are into it. And i think Anno's wasn't aware of this success. When he realized it ... he started to troll the rebuild with 2.0. Hopefully he make something like Miyazaki out of his franchise and studio. And his feelings (now that he made the Rebuild) is overwhelming desire to continue. Its like GRR Martin with the song of ice and fire .

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jun 25, 2014 6:59 am I'm sure he hates it and his jacuzzi full of $100 bills.

[]:             Quote Post by Black Swordsman » Wed Jul 02, 2014 12:34 pm Compiling_Autumn wrote: I would go as far to say that Anno has done some of the milking himself (Rebuild)

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Jul 02, 2014 1:03 pm Black Swordsman wrote: I wouldn't call 3 films in 7 years milking.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 02, 2014 2:16 pm He'll sell it to Disney. Asuka will become a Disney Princess™. Then the circle will be complete.
